title: Lebanon and Israel meet in Washington for tough negotiations under growing pressure
subhed: Direct talks begin Thursday between the Israeli government, which has little incentive to compromise, and Lebanese officials unable to disarm Hezbollah, as deadly escalation continues between the Israeli military and the Shiite movement.

title: Xi warns Trump that Taiwan could lead to 'conflict' at Beijing summit
With CMA Média, shipping magnate Rodolphe Saadé expands his empire
Slavery abolition commemorations remain source of discomfort for France's far right
subhed: Despite a 25-year-old law instituting annual commemorations of the history of slavery, far-right MPs are rarely present at these ceremonies. While Marine Le Pen now acknowledges France's responsibility for slavery, members of her party often prefers to emphasize the scale of the Arab-Muslim slave trade.

title: Sarkozy appeal trial: Prosecution calls for punishment 'commensurate with the gravity of the offenses'
subhed: The prosecution asked for the same sentence as in the initial trial: seven years in prison for Nicolas Sarkozy, as the 'instigator' and 'sole beneficiary' of a 'corruption pact' with the Libyan regime. Six and four-year sentences were requested for his associates Claude Guéant and Brice Hortefeux.

title: At the Cannes Film Festival, the art of criticizing without offending: 'It might be your most personal film yet'
subhed: With festival up and running, columnist Guillemette Faure explores the art of expressing an opinion without really stating it, so as not to upset anyone after a screening.

title: How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhed: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
subhed: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhed: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: How one family and their shipping company dominate Marseille
subhed: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (3/4). Based in France's second-largest city, the company led by Rodolphe Saadé plays a major role in the city but has become less visible since the controversy over excess profits.
